records.ety: [OE. comaunden, commanden, OF. comander, F. commander, fr. L. com- + mandare to commit to, to command. Cf. Commend, Mandate.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Commanded; p. pr. & vb. n. Commanding.]

defs: To order with authority; to lay injunction upon; to direct; to bid; to charge.
quotes: We are commanded to forgive our enemies, but you never read that we are commanded to forgive our friends.
Go to your mistress: Say, I command her come to me.

defs: To exercise direct authority over; to have control of; to have at one's disposal; to lead.
quotes: Such aid as I can spare you shall command.

defs: To have within a sphere of control, influence, access, or vision; to dominate by position; to guard; to overlook.
quotes: Bridges commanded by a fortified house.
Up to the eastern tower, Whose height commands as subject all the vale.
One side commands a view of the finest garden.

defs: To have power or influence of the nature of authority over; to obtain as if by ordering; to receive as a due; to challenge; to claim; as, justice commands the respect and affections of the people; the best goods command the best price.
quotes: 'Tis not in mortals to command success.

defs: To direct to come; to bestow.
quotes: I will command my blessing upon you.

defs: To have or to exercise direct authority; to govern; to sway; to influence; to give an order or orders.
quotes: And reigned, commanding in his monarchy.
For the king had so commanded concerning [Haman].

defs: To have a view, as from a superior position.
quotes: Far and wide his eye commands.

defs: An authoritative order requiring obedience; a mandate; an injunction.
quotes: Awaiting what command their mighty chief Had to impose.

defs: The possession or exercise of authority.
quotes: Command and force may often create, but can never cure, an aversion.

defs: Authority; power or right of control; leadership; as, the forces under his command.
Power to dominate, command, or overlook by means of position; scope of vision; survey.
quotes: The steepy stand Which overlooks the vale with wide command.

defs: Control; power over something; sway; influence; as, to have command over one's temper or voice; the fort has command of the bridge.
quotes: He assumed an absolute command over his readers.

defs: A body of troops, or any naval or military force or post, or the whole territory under the authority or control of a particular officer.
